title: allium durangoense
intro: Allium durangoense is a species of wild onion native to the Sierra Madre Occidental mountain range in northwestern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with Allium durangoense include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Allium durangoense typically involve the use of the plant's bulbs, which can be dried, crushed, and used to make teas, tinctures, or capsules.
